title: Patch Backport Branch Selection
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: patch backport branch selection.
assumptions: Initial facts reflect current tool-state: available branches, reviewers, CI jobs, environments, and approvers.
Approvals and CI validations are modeled as discrete boolean stages; external timing and retries are out of scope.
Release windows and approver availability are exclusive resources represented by availability predicates.
risks: Incorrect branch selection may introduce regressions into release branches if validation is insufficient.
Approval bottlenecks or unavailable approvers can delay mitigation under operational urgency.
CI job flakiness or environment instability may falsely block finalization and prolong incident resolution.
Misclassification of risk tags can cause inappropriate approval escalation or bypass of necessary checks.
